accept: Skill (~/.claude/skills/act/SKILL.md) has a section on the auto-mode push-blocking interaction with explicit settings.json snippet to allow it.
Snippet is tested in a project with auto-mode enabled — agent's git push origin main proceeds without halting.
Tradeoff is documented: auto-allowing push removes one safety net; the rationale is that act's loop already gates push behind a human-authorized close+commit cycle.
payload.description:
Discovered 2026-05-10 during act-1174 skill validation. Claude Code's auto-mode classifier blocks 'git push origin main' as a PR-bypass risk even when CLAUDE.md / the act skill say push is part of the canonical loop. Workaround for projects: a .claude/settings.json allow-rule for git push. The skill should mention this and provide the snippet so projects using act in auto mode aren't surprised by step 7 silently failing.

Two-part fix:
1. Document the harness behavior in the act skill's distribution / first-session section.
2. Provide a one-line .claude/settings.json snippet projects can drop to allow git push origin <default-branch> in auto mode.
payload.title: act skill should advise on Claude Code auto-mode push-blocking
